negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Video Phase 2 executor fees are not calculated from pixel-seconds with inference-step and FPS multipliers; the port lacks video Phase 2 fee calculators and baseline constants.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-async-fees/src/registry.rs reserves video pricing fields but reads them as zero and does not use them in fee math.
ritual-revm-internal/crates/precompile/src/video_call.rs calculates EVM gas from input/output byte lengths, not async executor fees from delivered duration, pixels, steps, or FPS.

notes: The only video-related code found is precompile gas accounting and zeroed reserved registry fields, neither of which implements the async fee invariant.
rationale: The new OutputUxParams and multiplier helpers feed into image_phase2_execution_fee and calculate_image_fees_phase2, adding steps-sensitive pricing to the existing pixel/minimum fee model. The video escrow and Phase 2 fee paths now apply both steps and FPS multipliers after the existing per-pixel-second fee calculation. Collapsed 2 closely related historical candidate statements into the latest effective formulation.
statement: Video Phase 2 executor fees must start from base_fee plus width * height * rounded_duration_seconds * per_pixel_second_fee, then apply num_inference_steps and fps multipliers normalized against VIDEO_STEPS_BASELINE and VIDEO_FPS_BASELINE, without reducing the fee when either parameter is zero or below baseline.
suggested_falsification_tests: Compute image Phase 2 fees for the same width and height with num_inference_steps 0, baseline, and 2x baseline; assert the first two equal the base fee and the 2x case doubles the executor fee.
Compute video Phase 2 fees for identical width, height, and duration with default UX params, 2x baseline steps, 2x baseline fps, and both; assert fees scale by the expected multiplicative factors.
summary: Video step/FPS-scaled Phase 2 pricing is not ported.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Compute image Phase 2 fees for the same width and height with num_inference_steps 0, baseline, and 2x baseline; assert the first two equal the base fee and the 2x case doubles the executor fee. Compute video Phase 2 fees for identical width, height, and duration with default UX params, 2x baseline steps, 2x baseline fps, and both; assert fees scale by the expected multiplicative factors.
title: Video pricing scales with steps and FPS
